title: What the top count shows
question: What should the decision count at the top of the viewer count?

history.from: Breakdown line read 'N logged' for the created-decision count
history.reason: Renamed the count to 'N created' — plainer than 'logged' for a non-technical reader, and clearer that it's the count of decisions created (vs revisions)

rationale: Headline the true total of decisions made — every created decision plus every revision counts as a decision that was made (d38) — then break it down on a second line: '60 decisions made / 38 created · 22 revisions · 1 open'. Chosen because the old top line counted only created decisions, so it undercounted the work; the breakdown keeps it honest about what's a revision.

options.label: Total made, with a breakdown line

tradeoffs.note: counts every decision-event, so the headline matches how much was actually decided
the second line spells out logged vs revisions vs open, so the big number isn't mistaken for file count
two lines instead of one short line

options.label: Just the logged decision count (the old way)

tradeoffs.note: counts only created decisions — revisions, which are also decisions made, are invisible
'60 decisions' with no breakdown reads as 60 separate decisions, hiding that 22 are revisions
